body:
## Summary
- Add an explicit `/update` route and `agent/update` label mapping to the existing `update-agent` skill.
- Add `agent-update.yml` for scheduled/manual Sepo update checks with schedule-policy gating and repository-scoped update runs.
- Update docs and focused route/workflow tests for the new update path.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/triage.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/resolve-dispatch-cli.test.js .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`
- `git diff --check`

Closes #94
Related #27

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777780598316 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjkwODEwMDk6aW1wbGVtZW50Ojk0OnJldmlldzoxMTU6Mw -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 94 | PARTIAL | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | The PR adds `update` as a first-class dispatch route, maps `/update` and `agent/update` to `update-agent`, and `applyDispatchPolicy` still applies `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` before route-specific handling. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | `agent-update.yml` uses separate `workflow_dispatch` inputs for `source_repo`, `source_ref`, `update_skills`, and `update_agent_md`, instead of overloading one command string. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The implementation reuses the existing `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`, router skill execution path, provider resolution action, scheduled activity gate, timeout resolver, and dispatch helpers. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR addresses #94 and related #27: explicit `/update`, default scheduled update check, near-biweekly cadence, and PR creation only when update changes exist. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Changes are scoped to route registration, dispatch policy/tests, one new workflow, update-skill invocation guidance, and matching docs. No unrelated refactor is visible in the PR diff. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The route is added by extending existing route sets and the existing skill job instead of introducing a separate router architecture. The scheduled workflow is straightforward and follows existing action composition. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | partial | 5/7 | Most route/workflow docs are updated, but `.agent/src/rubrics.ts` adds `update` to valid rubric routes while `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` still documents the `applies_to` route list without `update`. |

## Notes

- The strongest rubric alignment is reuse: this PR wires a new public route to the existing `update-agent` skill and existing workflow/action helpers.
- The main rubric-specific gap is a small docs sync miss in the rubrics documentation after adding `update` as a rubric-selectable route.
- No rubric-specific blocking issue found.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** Keep docs in sync: `.agent/src/rubrics.ts` adds `update` to `RUBRIC_ROUTE_NAMES`, but `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` still lists accepted `applies_to` values without `update`. Add `update` to that documented route list so rubric authors know the new route is valid.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #115 adds a first-class Sepo `update` route, mapping explicit `/update` and `agent/update` requests to the existing `update-agent` skill. It also adds a default `agent-update.yml` scheduled/manual workflow that checks for Sepo infrastructure updates and should only open a PR when changes exist. This addresses #94, relates to #27, and is part of the broader #107 user-facing distribution plan. The implementation generally reuses existing route, skill, provider, schedule-gate, and docs patterns.

## Review
Overall, the PR is close and has no blocking authorization or runtime design failure in the reviewed paths. The remaining issues are small but concrete workflow/docs fixes, plus one optional regression-test gap.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Manual update workflow can run from a non-default ref | WARNING | Manual `workflow_dispatch` runs can be launched with `--ref`, but checkout follows that ref while the prompt says the target is the default branch. |
| Route documentation inventories are incomplete | WARNING | Rubrics route docs omit `update`, and the touched developer-notes slash-route list still omits `/orchestrate`. |
| Missing explicit update access-policy regression test | INFO | Existing authorization logic appears correct, but there is no focused test proving `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` can restrict the new `update` route. |

## Progress
- Existing reviewer outputs agree the route is explicit-only and reuses the existing `update-agent` skill path.
- The rubrics review already posted the `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` docs-sync warning, so I did not duplicate it inline.
- I posted inline comments for the manual checkout-ref issue and the touched developer-notes route list.
- Codex review verification reported `npm --prefix .agent test` passing with 481 tests.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Manual update workflow can run from a non-default ref</summary>

**Cause:** `actions/checkout@v4` in `.github/workflows/agent-update.yml` has no `ref`, so manual dispatch can check out a non-default ref while the update prompt advertises `${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}`.

**Candidate solutions:** Pin checkout to `ref: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}` so manual and scheduled update checks operate from the same default-branch baseline.

**Comments:** This is not a scheduled-run issue, but it can make manual update runs audit and branch from the wrong checkout.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Route documentation inventories are incomplete</summary>

**Cause:** `.agent/src/rubrics.ts` now accepts `update` in `RUBRIC_ROUTE_NAMES`, but `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` still omits it from the documented `applies_to` route list. Also, `.agent/docs/technical-details/developer-notes.md` was updated for `/update` but still omits existing `/orchestrate`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `update` to the rubrics docs route list and add `/orchestrate` to the developer-notes hardcoded slash-route list.

**Comments:** The rubrics-docs issue is already covered by the existing rubrics review comment; only the developer-notes issue needed a new inline comment.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing explicit update access-policy regression test</summary>

**Cause:** The new route relies on the shared top-level `applyDispatchPolicy` access check, but tests only cover immediate explicit `update`, implicit rejection, and existing route restriction examples.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a focused test mirroring the existing explicit `implement` restriction test, using an access policy that denies or narrows `update`.

**Comments:** This is coverage-only; reviewers did not identify a live authorization bypass.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved workflow and docs findings are concrete branch changes and are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Pin `.github/workflows/agent-update.yml` checkout to `${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}`.
- [ ] Add `update` to `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` documented `applies_to` route values.
- [ ] Add `/orchestrate` to `.agent/docs/technical-details/developer-notes.md` hardcoded slash-route list.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

- Pinned [`agent-update.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-94/codex-25269081009/.github/workflows/agent-update.yml) checkout to the repository default branch for manual dispatch consistency.
- Synced route inventories in [`rubrics.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-94/codex-25269081009/.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md) and [`developer-notes.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/implement-issue-94/codex-25269081009/.agent/docs/technical-details/developer-notes.md).
- Checks passed: `git diff --check`; `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

PR fixes succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777781418813 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjk0NzY0NzE6Zml4X3ByOjExNTpyZXZpZXc6MTE1OjU -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow/generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | `/update` is added as a first-class route and still flows through dispatch policy before the shared skill/update job; `resolve-dispatch` maps it to `update-agent` only after route resolution. Evidence: [triage.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b3afa8d34edc19badc1a18f720152f8188f5b797/.agent/src/triage.ts), [resolve-dispatch.ts](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b3afa8d34edc19badc1a18f720152f8188f5b797/.agent/src/cli/resolve-dispatch.ts), [agent-router.yml](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b3afa8d34edc19badc1a18f720152f8188f5b797/.github/workflows/agent-router.yml). |
| coding_workflow/generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | `agent-update.yml` uses separate typed `workflow_dispatch` inputs for `source_repo`, `source_ref`, `update_skills`, and `update_agent_md`; it does not encode multiple update options into one command string. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR wires `/update`, `agent/update`, and the scheduled workflow to the existing `.skills/update-agent/SKILL.md`, existing provider resolution, scheduled gate, timeout, envelope, and run-agent-task paths. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The implementation matches #94 and related #27/#107 context: explicit update route, near-biweekly scheduled check, reuse of update skills, and PR creation only when update changes exist. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is scoped to route registration, dispatch handling/tests, the new update workflow, the update skill instructions, and matching docs. No unrelated refactor is visible. |
| coding_style/generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The route is added by extending existing route lists and the existing skill execution path, while the scheduled workflow follows the project’s standard action composition. |
| coding_workflow/generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | The earlier docs-sync gap is fixed at current head: `update` now appears in rubrics docs, developer notes, supported workflows, quick start, key concepts, AGENT.md, README, and agent action docs. |

## Notes

- The current PR head includes the follow-up fixes from the prior review: default-branch checkout is pinned in `agent-update.yml`, rubrics route docs include `update`, and developer notes include both `/update` and `/orchestrate`.
- I did not score selected rubrics that do not apply to this PR’s implementation surface, including merge strategy, preview contracts, stateful persistence failures, rubric authoring, and accepted workflow stops.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings at current PR head.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #115 adds a first-class Sepo `update` route, mapping `/update` and `agent/update` to the existing `update-agent` skill. It also adds `agent-update.yml`, a scheduled/manual workflow intended to check installed Sepo infrastructure and open an update PR only when changes exist. The work closes #94, relates to #27’s near-biweekly “not too annoying” update-check request, and is part of the broader #107 distribution plan. Current reviewers agree the core route, access-policy path, reuse of the skill infrastructure, and docs coverage are generally sound.

## Review
Overall, the PR is close: no reviewer found a blocking route authorization or runtime design issue. The remaining concern is one concrete scheduled-workflow behavior gap, with a few INFO-level cleanup or coverage notes.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Scheduled updater can duplicate a pending update PR | WARNING | The new workflow tells the skill to use its default date-based update branch, so a later scheduled run can recreate the same update as another PR while an earlier one is still open. |
| Rubric prompt examples still omit `update` | INFO | `update` is now a valid rubric route, but the rubric initialization/update prompt schema examples still list the older route set. |
| Optional update route hardening notes | INFO | Claude noted low-risk gaps: no dedicated `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` denial test for `update`, and redundant `update-agent` ternaries in the router despite `resolve-dispatch` already emitting the skill. |

## Progress
- Earlier warnings about default-branch checkout and route documentation were fixed in the current PR head.
- The current rubrics review is PASS with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Existing inline comments already cover the older checkout/docs issues, so I did not duplicate them.
- I posted one new inline comment on `.github/workflows/agent-update.yml` line 127 for the duplicate pending update PR risk.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Scheduled updater can duplicate a pending update PR</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow prompt says `update branch: use the update-agent skill default`; reviewers identified that default as date-based. If an update PR remains open, the next 1st/15th run can compare against the unchanged default branch, see the same diff, and open another dated PR.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a pending-update guard in [`agent-update.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/b3afa8d34edc19badc1a18f720152f8188f5b797/.github/workflows/agent-update.yml#L127) or in the `update-agent` skill. Reuse or report an existing open `agent/update-agent-infra-*` PR/branch before creating a new branch.

**Comments:** This matters because #27 explicitly raised the “too annoying” concern. Rubrics/Claude did not mark this as blocking, while Codex elevated it to WARNING based on the scheduled-update acceptance context.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Rubric prompt examples still omit `update`</summary>

**Cause:** `.agent/src/rubrics.ts` and `.agent/docs/architecture/rubrics.md` now include `update`, but `.github/prompts/rubrics-initialization.md` and `.github/prompts/rubrics-update.md` still show schema examples without it.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `update` to those prompt examples’ `applies_to` route lists.

**Comments:** This is not runtime-blocking and was not called out by the rubrics reviewer, but it can steer future rubric-writing agents away from a valid route.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Optional update route hardening notes</summary>

**Cause:** The shared access-policy path appears correct, but there is no focused test proving `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` can restrict `update`. In `agent-router.yml`, the router also repeats `needs.portal.outputs.route == 'update' && 'update-agent' || needs.portal.outputs.skill` even though the portal already emits `skill=update-agent`.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a small update-specific denial test mirroring the existing implement restriction test. Optionally simplify the router to use the emitted `skill` output.

**Comments:** These were Claude INFO notes only. They do not change the recommended automation action.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The duplicate pending update PR guard is a concrete branch change and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add a pending-update guard so scheduled `/update` runs reuse/report an existing open update PR or branch instead of creating duplicate dated PRs.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 6/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777782009972 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyNjk1MjIyMzk6cmV2aWV3OjExNTpmaXhfcHI6MTE1OjY -->

**Sepo did not produce code changes for this PR.**

Please add more context or restate the requested fixes, then try again.

Based on the latest open PR, the plan should stay on PR #115 first.

**Current State**
- Latest open PR: [#115 Add Sepo update route and scheduled workflow](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/115).
- It handles #94 / #27 under the broader [#107 distribution plan](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/107).
- Earlier checkout/docs feedback was already fixed.
- Latest review synthesis now has one actionable item: prevent scheduled update runs from creating duplicate pending update PRs.

**Immediate Plan**
1. Finish PR #115 before starting another workstream.
2. Add a pending-update guard to `agent-update.yml` before provider/runtime setup.
3. Guard behavior:
   - Look for an open same-repo PR whose head branch starts with `agent/update-agent-infra-`.
   - If found, skip the update agent run and report the existing PR URL in the workflow summary.
   - If none exists, proceed with the scheduled update check.
4. Keep the logic deterministic and testable, preferably in a small `.agent/src/cli/*` helper rather than embedding nontrivial GitHub API logic directly in workflow YAML.
5. Add focused coverage proving the guard runs before expensive provider/runtime setup and skips when a pending update PR exists.
6. Run focused checks: build if source changes, relevant node tests, YAML parsing if workflow changed, and `git diff --check`.
7. Send PR #115 back through review.

**After PR #115 Is Clean**
1. Merge #115 using the repo’s normal merge flow.
2. Return to #107 and pick the next workstream.
3. Suggested order:
   - Onboarding/install automation: follow up on PR #62 discussion about GitHub App installation webhook bootstrap.
   - Repo-specific update signal/badge: clarify scope, then implement only after update workflow is stable.
   - Failure/bug reporting: implement #26 as Discussion-based intake with dedupe, not auto-created issues.

I would not start #26 or the install webhook work until #115’s duplicate-update risk is fixed, because #115 is the active latest PR and directly affects the update/distribution foundation.

Sepo orchestration stopped after `fix-pr` concluded `no_changes`.

- Source action: `fix-pr`
- Source conclusion: `no_changes`
- Target: `pull_request #115`
- Round: `6/10`
- Reason: fix-pr concluded no_changes; no automatic handoff was dispatched because fix-pr must succeed before re-review
- Source run ID: `25269695494`

No follow-up workflow was dispatched. Inspect the source action status comment and workflow logs before retrying or continuing manually.

<!-- sepo-agent-orchestrate-stop -->

title: Add Sepo update route and scheduled workflow
